input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction UQ present but not validated.

case_context.cou: the calibrated wide band informs a margin decision the engineer can act on
case_context.decision_consequence: margin set from the calibrated band

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The UQ-validation gap the pattern describes is absent: the intervals are wide but calibrated (coverage matches nominal in the COU regime, epistemic propagated), so the honest large uncertainty is complete evidence — there is no defeater to act on.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: intervals wide but calibrated; coverage 0.90 vs nominal 0.90; epistemic propagated

forbidden_claims: restricts or rejects on the basis of wide-but-calibrated intervals (over-action)
asserts the surrogate is credible/correct (a verdict the package does not adjudicate)

hardness.obvious_signal: the prediction intervals are very WIDE — the model looks alarmingly uncertain
hardness.conflicting_signal: the wide intervals are HONEST: empirical coverage in the COU's regime matches nominal (0.90), epistemic uncertainty is propagated, so the large-but-calibrated uncertainty is exactly what should be reported

label_provenance.external_grounding: Honest, calibrated large uncertainty is the desired UQ outcome (arXiv:2007.08792); over-acting on calibrated width is the over-action error a control must catch.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
